title: Cannot run systemd inside container
body:
podman version 4.2.1
Fedora 36 Linux fedora 5.17.5-300.fc36.x86_64 #

cmd: 
```
podman --root=/data/storage run --user=root --mount type=bind,source=/usb/data/,target=/home/data -td localhost/my_test
```

Error:

```
fedora setroubleshoot[1586]: SELinux is preventing init from read access on the file /usr/lib/systemd/libsystemd-shared-250.so.
                                             
                                             *****  Plugin restorecon (54.2 confidence) suggests   ************************
                                             
                                             If you want to fix the label. 
                                             /usr/lib/systemd/libsystemd-shared-250.so default label should be lib_t.
                                             Then you can run restorecon. The access attempt may have been stopped due to insufficient permissions to access a parent directory in which case try to change the following command accordingly.
                                             Do
                                             # /sbin/restorecon -v /usr/lib/systemd/libsystemd-shared-250.so
                                             
                                             *****  Plugin file (16.6 confidence) suggests   ******************************
                                             
                                             This is caused by a newly created file system.
                                             Then you need to add labels to it.
                                             Do
                                             /sbin/restorecon -R -v /usr/lib/systemd/libsystemd-shared-250.so
                                             
                                             *****  Plugin file (16.6 confidence) suggests   ******************************
                                             
                                             If you think this is caused by a badly mislabeled machine.
                                             Then you need to fully relabel.
                                             Do
                                             touch /.autorelabel; reboot
                                             
                                             *****  Plugin catchall_labels (3.18 confidence) suggests   *******************
                                             
                                             If you want to allow init to have read access on the libsystemd-shared-250.so file
                                             Then you need to change the label on /usr/lib/systemd/libsystemd-shared-250.so
                                             Do
                                             # semanage fcontext -a -t FILE_TYPE '/usr/lib/systemd/libsystemd-shared-250.so'
                                             where FILE_TYPE is one of the following: NetworkManager_tmp_t, abrt_helper_exec_t, abrt_tmp_t, abrt_upload_watch_tmp_t, abrt_var_cache_t, abrt_var_run_t, admin_crontab_tmp_t, afs_cache_t, alsa_tmp_t, amanda_tmp_t, antivirus_tmp_t, apcupsd_tmp_t, apmd_tmp_t, arpwatch_tmp_t, asterisk_tmp_t, auditadm_sudo_tmp_t, auditd_tmp_t, automount_tmp_t,                                              Then execute:
                                             restorecon -v '/usr/lib/systemd/libsystemd-shared-250.so'
                                             
                                             
                                             *****  Plugin catchall (1.03 confidence) suggests   **************************
                                             
                                             If you believe that init should be allowed read access on the libsystemd-shared-250.so file by default.
                                             Then you should report this as a bug.
                                             You can generate a local policy module to allow this access.
                                             Do
                                             allow this access for now by executing:
                                             # ausearch -c 'init' --raw | audit2allow -M my-init
                                             # semodule -X 300 -i my-init.pp
```

comments.body:
Could you please attach the AVCs.

sudo ausearch -m avc
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since I never heard back, I am closing this PR.
